description:
Mechanical application of the pattern proven in fz-duq.2. Depends on
fz-duq.2 landing first (helpers + lower_if + golden-CLIF discipline
all proven there).

## Replace `lower_case` (`src/ir_lower.rs:1854`)

Same two-shape pattern as lower_if:

- **Tail position:** allocate try_blocks + fail_block, lower each
  clause body with `is_tail=true`, finalize with `Return(v)` if not
  already terminated. No join fn.
- **Non-tail position:** mint a join fn upfront via
  `branching_join_fn`. Each clause body lowered with `is_tail=true`,
  finalize with `TailCall(join_fn, [v, ...captured])` if not already
  terminated. After all clauses, `switch_to_join_fn` and return
  `join_param`.

Pattern-bind code (lower_pattern_bind) stays the same — it operates
within the try_block and only uses the per-block `next` BlockId for
mismatch routing (these `next` blocks are reached only by direct
Goto from within the same fn, so they don't have the CPS-split
hazard).

The synthetic `case_clause` halt at `fail_block` stays as-is — it's
a self-terminating Halt, no join needed.

## Test

Add a case-with-call-in-clause-body test mirroring fz-duq.2's repros:

```fz
fn helper(), do: 7
fn classify(n) do
  case n do
    0 -> helper()
    _ -> 99
  end
end
fn main() do
  print(classify(0))
  print(classify(5))
end
```

Expected stdout: `7\n99\n`. Today silently broken via the same Bug 2.

## Acceptance

- `cargo test --workspace` green.
- `cargo test --workspace golden_clif` zero drift.
- New case-fixture passes via interp + JIT.

## Removes

- `lower_case`'s `join_b` allocation (`src/ir_lower.rs:1874-1875`).
- `lower_case`'s `Goto(join_b, ...)` at `:1910`.
- Per-clause `saved_env` / `saved_order` / `ctx.terminated = false`
  boilerplate now subsumed by the arm-finalizer helper.
title: fz-duq.3: lower_case uses continuation-fn join
